title: Rust property tests for next_available_note_id are blocked on todo!() Smart Constructors and never actually verify PROP-003/PROP-022 in CI
description: promptnotes/src-tauri/src/domain/vault/note_id.rs:27-35 constructs Timestamp and NoteId via `try_from_epoch_millis(...).unwrap()` and `NoteId::try_new(...).unwrap()`. The Timestamp::try_from_epoch_millis impl in docs/domain/code/rust/src/value_objects.rs:68-74 is concrete, but Tag::try_new (line 99-101), Frontmatter::try_new (line 157-163), and VaultPath::try_new (line 213-215) are still `todo!('Phase 11+ 実装')`. The PROP-003/PROP-022 proptests (lines 132-178) generate strings matching the regex and call NoteId::try_new — that constructor does exist (line 31). However the comments at lines 24-30 and 14-16 describe these as 'Phase 2a Red phase tests' that will panic until Phase 2b. There is no recorded green-phase evidence in this manifest that these Rust tests pass — the manifest lists `note_id.rs` under both `tests` and `source` (manifest.json:19,30). Consequently PROP-003 (required: true) is asserted only by the TS implementation in promptnotes/src/lib/domain/app-startup/initialize-capture.ts; the Rust property tests provide no additional verification.
rationale: Phase 2c (refactor) should either (a) remove the Rust note_id.rs tests from the Sprint 1 sources/tests until the Rust crate is wired up, or (b) confirm Cargo test execution evidence and include it as a Phase-2b green log. As-is, listing them in the manifest implies they verify PROP-003/PROP-022, which they don't unless they actually run.
